summary: Muddy audience recording that still captures a stellar 1973 Boulder show with killer China > Rider transition and powerful Weir performances.
review: The performance is widely praised – the first set is solid, the second set “killer,” and songs like China Cat Sunflower → New Riders of the Purple Sage and the Promised Land > Bertha > The Greatest sequence get special shout‑outs.  The tape quality is uneven, with many reviewers calling it cloudy or muddy, though a few note moments that sound almost like a soundboard source.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance (standout songs, good set flow) outweighs the inconsistent audience recording quality, resulting in an overall solid rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Many reviewers describe the audio as muddy, cloudy, or ‘not a great AUD’, though some note occasional clarity that sounds almost like a soundboard.

standout_songs: New Riders of the Purple Sage
show_quality.setlist_flow: Setlists are described as freer and well‑structured, with a very good first set and a killer second set; transitions such as China > Rider are highlighted as sublime.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar work is praised as peaking and the China>Rider transition showcases his playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir is noted to be on – strong vocals/guitar that please Weir fans.
